design_summary: Three justified candidate families, each a BOUNDED preregistered correspondence (phoneme inventory + allowed changes into the LA syllabary + a SMALL lexicon of candidate-language WORDS). Each lexeme is expanded to its predicted LA syllabogram-value string(s) via the FIXED correspondence (never read off LA). A held-out LA multi-sign word MATCHES a family iff its value-string is a predicted whole-word form, or (for affix entries) it ends with a predicted affix string. Primary statistic = token-weighted match rate on the held-out multi-sign syllabic word set. Scored vs the WP6 multi-family end-to-end null: (S) order-shuffle, (W) wrong-language opaque LB length-matched, (R) random-prior (lexicon permuted, search size preserved). Decisive nulls for a LANGUAGE claim = W and R. FWER per family = fraction of decisive null realizations with match >= real. Search-adjusted across the 3 families by Holm. Leave-one-lexeme-out reported for robustness.
non_circularity: Correspondences map candidate-language PHONEMES to syllabogram VALUES; predicted forms are DERIVED from candidate-language dictionary words, not fitted to LA. LB values only ever appear as the wrong-language NULL corpus. The conventional LA transliteration is a fixed published convention applied identically to every corpus and family.
bounded_no_root_search: Lexicons are fixed a priori (6 entries/family), from cited proposals; no unbounded thesaurus search. The combinatorial expansion size (effective_n predicted forms) is reported and is automatically deflated because every null realization runs the SAME expansion/matcher.
syllabogram_decomposition_note: Each AB syllabogram is decomposed to (onset_consonant, vowel) by standard Ventris-Chadwick values in the runner. Pure-vowel signs have empty onset.

ANA_preGreek_Anatolian.gloss: Pre-Greek Aegean / Anatolian-contact (Luwic). Sources: L.R. Palmer; M. Finkelberg (2001); Woudhuizen; the pre-Greek toponymic -ss-/-nth- substrate; Hittite/Luwian ishassara-.

lexicon.phonemes: h i s a s a r a
t a r h u n t

SEM_westSemitic.gloss: West/Common Semitic. Sources: C.H. Gordon (1966, Evidence for the Minoan Language); J. Best (1972); Best & Woudhuizen (1988). Proto/Common-Semitic admin/commodity/name roots derived through the fixed coarse correspondence.

CTRL_agnostic_random.gloss: Agnostic negative control: a fixed random lexicon of matched size/length-structure, no linguistic motivation. Expected FWER ~ uniform; verifies the pipeline is not rigged to pass.
CTRL_agnostic_random.lexicon: GENERATED at runtime: 6 random entries (4 word + 2 suffix) with lengths matched to the mean of ANA/SEM, phonemes drawn uniformly from this inventory under fixed seed 20260708.

held_out_target: GORILA word units, syllabic-only, multi-sign (>=2 signs): 973 tokens / discriminating set. Single-sign words excluded (non-discriminating for lexical hypotheses).
primary_statistic: token-weighted match rate = sum token-freq(matching words)/total tokens, on the multi-sign held-out set.
decision_rule: A family CLEARS iff Holm-adjusted decisive FWER (max over the W and R per-null empirical p) < 0.05 AND the leave-one-lexeme-out decisive FWER stays < 0.05 (not driven by a single lexeme).
prior_expectation: WP6 was AT_END_TO_END_NULL (wrong-language LB scored higher). Expectation is NULL; run properly and report which family, if any, clears.
